title: Topic-Matched Different Voice
description: Baseline and output discuss the same packet, date, and exhibit, but the cadence surfaces diverge. This guards against treating shared topic as protected authorship contact.

inputs.protectedBaseline: EXHIBIT-42 keeps returning as a little clock in the middle of the file, and 6/13 keeps sounding like the hinge where the record learns to breathe. I do not want the packet rushed; I want it witnessed, marked, and carried without flattening the story inside it.
inputs.maskReference: Packet first. Date second. Mark the file. Keep the chain legible. No extra narrative.
inputs.messageDraft: Please mark EXHIBIT-42 and route the 6/13 custody note into local review.
inputs.protectedOutput: Mark EXHIBIT-42. Add the 6/13 custody note. Route the packet to local review. Keep the chain legible and stop there.

calibrationNotes: The topic trap appears when shared nouns, dates, and exhibit markers inflate style contact.
The report should remain conservative and limitation-rich.
